Helping Young Families, Plan Their Financial Future

More Than Returns! It is
Financial Freedom for your family...
Octaraa thrives to empower young families to plan, invest and achieve their family's financial goals easily, because we truly believe investing gives you and your family the freedom to make better choices. Today and in the future.

Will Double Every Eight Years

How much will it really cost to put your child through college?
Calculate Now
*As per an Economic Times survey, the cost of education increases by 10% every year.

Contact Now

Welcome to Octaraa
Helping Families Build Wealth with Clarity and Confidence
Octaraa is a family-first wealth platform built for modern Indian families who value clarity, transparency, and long-term financial growth. We simplify wealth creation by bringing together investments, financial insights, and planning tools in a unified experience so families can grow, manage, and secure their future together.

The Gap
20 years of missed compounding due to lack of financial education.

The Realization
Biased advice and unsuitable products like ULIPs eroded value.

The Solution
Octaraa: Unbiased, transparent, and family-first.

Our Story: Built from Experience
Octaraa was born from a simple realisation most of us start managing money far too late, often with the wrong guidance. After nearly two decades in the corporate world, our founder began investing only in 2019, navigating a landscape filled with complexity, bias, and products that didn't truly serve long-term goals. That experience exposed two critical gaps: the cost of delayed investing, and a system driven more by commissions than client needs. More importantly, it revealed a deeper problem families lack access to clear, trustworthy financial guidance when they need it most. Octaraa exists to change that helping young families start early, make informed decisions, and build wealth with confidence.

FAQ - Who is the founder of Octaraa?
Vaibhav Jain is the Founder & CEO of Octaraa.

Meet The Founder
"Missing compounding is India's biggest hidden financial loss." That belief is deeply personal for Vaibhav. For over 20 years, Vaibhav Jain built a successful corporate career working with global companies like Oracle, Microsoft, VMware, SAP, and later Google Cloud. But when it came to his own money, there was one big gap: for two decades, Vaibhav stayed away from investing. The world of finance felt complex, overwhelming, and difficult to trust. When he finally took his first step in 2019, it turned into a setback because a bank advisor, driven by commissions, mis-sold him a ULIP plan he never truly needed. That moment wasn't just a bad financial decision. It was a realization. The real loss wasn't the product. It was 20 years of missed compounding. From Regret to Purpose. That experience changed everything. Vaibhav realized his story wasn't unique. Across India, millions of families were making financial decisions without clarity, relying on biased advice & unknowingly losing years of wealth creation. And that's where the idea for Octaraa was born. Octaraa is built on a simple but powerful belief: People don't need more financial products. They need clarity, trust, and guidance.

Our Mission and Vision
We're on a mission to bring clarity and confidence to every Indian family's financial journey through simple, trusted solutions that help them grow wealth without confusion or bias.

Understand
See your family's complete financial picture: goals, risks, and opportunities all in one place.

Decide
Make smarter financial decisions with clear, unbiased guidance aligned to your family's real-life goals.

Grow
Build long-term financial security and wealth for and with your family consistently and confidently.

What We Offer
Octaraa provides everything your family needs to manage, grow and understand wealth together in one place.

Holistic Wealth Dashboard
Get a complete view of your family's net worth by tracking all investments in one place even those held elsewhere.

Ask Samaira AI
From portfolio queries to financial concepts, visit Samaira AI for instant, unbiased answers and insights.

Access a range of investment options carefully aligned to your family's life goals.

Financial Planning Calculators
Make confident decisions with Octaraa's calculators for SIPs, retirement, education planning, and more built to simplify complex financial choices for your family.
Note: Some Financial Assets & features are coming soon on the platform

Our Commitment To Transparency
We are more than just a platform, we are your partners in securing a prosperous future for the next generation.

Built for Privacy & Security
Your financial data is protected with bank-grade encryption and strict privacy controls.

Future Fees Prediction Calculator
Calculate the future cost of education by factoring in inflation and plan your investments accordingly to meet your child's educational goals. Example: Current Annual Fees ₹1,00,000, Expected Inflation Rate, Years Until Admission -> Future Fees ₹2,15,892. To achieve this target, start SIP of ₹939/month (assuming 12% annual returns). Disclaimer: This is just a mathematical calculation based on some historical assumptions and might not represent the actual real life scenario.

Privacy Policy
DHANSAMRIDDHI FINVEST PRIVATE LIMITED is committed to protecting your personal data. Effective: 1st May 2026. Version 1.0. Governed by the laws of the Republic of India.

Data Fiduciary Details
Legal Entity: DHANSAMRIDDHI FINVEST PRIVATE LIMITED. CIN: U64199HR2025PTC139592. AMFI Reg. No.: 350268. GST Registration: 06AAMCD2783L1Z6. Grievance / DPO Email: connect@octaraa.com. Grievance Officer: Mr. Vaibhav Jain. Registered Address: Building No. 44, Ground Floor, Sector-32, Gurgaon, Sadar Bazar, Haryana, India, 122001.

Data Security Compliance
TLS 1.3 encryption for all data in transit; AES-256 encryption for all Personal Data and SPDI stored at rest; Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) mandatory for all User account access; Role-Based Access Control (RBAC); Data masking and tokenisation for PAN, Aadhaar, bank account numbers; Web Application Firewall (WAF), DDoS protection, IDS/IPS; Security Operations Centre (SOC) 24x7; Annual VAPT by CERT-In empanelled auditors.

Cross-Border Transfer and Data Localisation
Your Personal Data is primarily stored and processed within India. Payment transaction data is stored EXCLUSIVELY in India; PMLA/AML/STR/CTR records stored in India; Aadhaar authentication logs stored within UIDAI infrastructure, never exported.

Samaira AI - Automated Processing Provisions
Conversations with Samaira AI are stored and may be reviewed by data science and compliance teams for quality assurance, model improvement, and regulatory compliance purposes. Samaira AI generates recommendations based on information provided by you and market data. AI outputs are advisory and informational only - they are NOT a substitute for professional investment advice. All investment decisions remain solely your responsibility and are subject to market risks. Data used to train or fine-tune AI models is irreversibly anonymised or pseudonymised prior to such use. You have the right to request human review of any AI-generated recommendation.

Example: Monthly Investment, Expected Annual Return, Investment Period -> Total Invested ₹6,00,000, Estimated Returns ₹5,50,193, Maturity Value ₹11,50,193. Recurring Deposits Calculator
Calculate growth on recurring deposits to optimize your savings and achieve financial goals. Example: Total Deposited ₹3,00,000, Interest Earned ₹53,370, Maturity Amount ₹3,53,370. Disclaimer: This is just a mathematical calculation based on some historical assumptions and might not represent the actual real life scenario.

SWP Calculator
Plan your systematic withdrawals from a lump sum investment and see how long your corpus will last. An SWP, or Systematic Withdrawal Plan, allows you to withdraw a fixed amount of money at regular intervals from your mutual fund investments. It is the reverse of a SIP. Instead of investing regularly, you redeem units from your mutual fund scheme periodically to receive steady income. Useful for retirees or those needing regular cash flow. Key benefits: Regular income stream, capital appreciation potential (remaining investment continues to grow), tax efficiency (only capital gains taxed, not entire withdrawal), flexibility (adjust amount/frequency or stop), rupee cost averaging in reverse. Mechanism: units equivalent to withdrawal amount are redeemed at the prevailing NAV on the chosen date; number of units redeemed varies with NAV. Best suited for: retirees, individuals seeking regular income, those wanting tax-efficient withdrawals, people with a lump sum investment. Disclaimer: This is just a mathematical calculation based on some historical assumptions and might not represent the actual real life scenario.

XIRR Calculator
Calculate the annualised return on investments with multiple cash flows, ideal for SIPs and staggered investments. Example: Total Invested ₹1,80,000, Wealth Gained ₹70,000, Maturity Value ₹2,50,000. Disclaimer: This is just a mathematical calculation based on some historical assumptions and might not represent the actual real life scenario.

Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana Calculator
Estimate returns on Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) investments to plan for your daughter's future financial security. Current SSY Interest Rate: 8.2% p.a. Min ₹250, Max ₹1,50,000 yearly; girl's current age must be 10 or below. Example: Total Investment ₹7,50,000, Total Interest Earned ₹15,59,193, Maturity Value ₹23,09,193. Deposits up to ₹1,50,000/year qualify for 80C deduction. Interest & maturity are fully tax-exempt (EEE). Disclaimer: This is just a mathematical calculation based on some historical assumptions and might not represent the actual real life scenario.

PPF Calculator
Calculate returns on your Public Provident Fund (PPF) investments to plan for long-term financial goals with tax benefits. Current rate 7.1% p.a. Example: Total Investment ₹22,50,000, Total Interest Earned ₹15,48,515, Maturity Value ₹37,98,515. Disclaimer: This is just a mathematical calculation based on some historical assumptions and might not represent the actual real life scenario.

Income Tax Calculator
Compare Old vs New tax regime and calculate your total tax liability, cess, and net take-home pay for FY 2025-26. Assessment Year 2026-2027. New Regime Slabs (AY 2026-2027): up to 4,00,000; 4,00,000-8,00,000; 8,00,000-12,00,000; 12,00,000-16,00,000; 16,00,000-20,00,000; 20,00,000-24,00,000; above 24,00,000. Disclaimer: This is just a mathematical calculation based on some historical assumptions and might not represent the actual real life scenario.

Calculate the Compound Annual Growth Rate of your investments to measure and compare performance over time. Example: Initial Investment ₹1,00,000, Total Returns ₹1,00,000, Return Percentage 100%, CAGR 14.87%. Retirement Calculator
Plan your retirement corpus and calculate the monthly savings needed to retire comfortably. Inputs: Current Age, Monthly Expenses, Retirement Age, Retirement Lifestyle (Like a King / I Am Happy / Like a Monk), Investment Strategy (Safe: PF/FD etc, or Aggressive: MF/Equity etc). Example: Total Invested ₹30,96,000, Corpus Required ₹3,77,54,695, Monthly Savings Needed ₹8,600. Disclaimer: This is just a mathematical calculation based on some historical assumptions and might not represent the actual real life scenario.

Combining Insurance and Investments: Smart Move or Costly Mistake?
Many people prefer financial products that combine investment and insurance into one plan (ULIPs, endowment plans, money-back policies) because they seem simple and convenient. However, convenience does not always mean financial efficiency. Issues: 1. Limited Flexibility - insurance needs and investment goals change over time but combined products may not adapt well. 2. Lower Transparency - difficult to understand insurance costs vs investment charges vs actual returns. 3. Potentially Lower Investment Efficiency - investment-linked insurance may not match returns of dedicated mutual funds/SIPs. 4. Insurance and Investing Have Different Purposes - insurance is for protection, investing is for wealth creation; combining reduces effectiveness of each and often costs more. A Smarter Approach: separate insurance for protection (term insurance) from investments for wealth creation (SIPs/mutual funds, FDs for stable savings) - provides better flexibility, transparency, and financial control. FAQ: Is combining investment and insurance a good idea? Depends on goals, but separate products often provide better flexibility and clarity. Examples of combined products: ULIPs, endowment plans, money-back policies. Why do people choose combined products? They appear simple and convenient. A more structured approach: insurance for protection + separate investments for wealth creation.

Why People Check Portfolios Daily | The Psychology Behind Constant Investing Anxiety
Many investors repeatedly track market movements, mutual funds, and stock prices even when investing for the long term, driven by emotions like fear, uncertainty, excitement, and need for control. Reasons: 1. Fear of Loss - losses feel more painful than gains. 2. Need for Control - monitoring feels like control but increases stress. 3. Easy Access to Apps - instant notifications cause emotional reactions to normal fluctuations. 4. Short-Term Thinking - shifts focus from long-term goals. Effects: increases stress/anxiety, encourages emotional decisions (panic selling), distracts from long-term goals, reduces investing discipline. Recommendations: focus on long-term goals, review periodically (monthly/quarterly, not daily), accept market volatility as normal, stay consistent (e.g. via SIPs). FAQ: Is checking portfolios daily bad? Not always, but excessive checking increases stress. How often should long-term investors check? Monthly or quarterly is usually enough. Why do investors panic during drops? Emotional biases like fear and loss aversion. Can emotional investing affect returns? Yes, often leads to poor long-term outcomes.

Best Financial Planning Websites in India | Top Platforms for Smarter Money Management
Comparison of financial planning platforms in India. Octaraa - Best for Family-Centric Financial Planning: family dashboard and family tree structure, goal-based financial planning, financial calculators, FD booking, financial literacy blogs. ET Money - SIP/mutual fund investing, expense tracking, tax-saving, insurance, portfolio tracking; best for SIP investing and expense tracking. PersonalFN - mutual fund research and ratings, investment advisory content, portfolio analysis; best for mutual fund research and investment education. Investing.com India - live market data, financial news, investment tracking, economic calendar; best for market updates and financial news. SEBI Investor Tools - official financial planning calculators and educational resources; best for goal planning and beginner financial education. A good financial planning website should: simplify decisions via structured tools, support long-term goal planning (retirement, education, wealth creation), improve financial literacy, help users stay organized with dashboards/calculators, encourage disciplined rational decision-making. For families specifically, Octaraa stands out for goal-based planning, structured financial management, financial literacy content, and family-focused tools.

A Mutual Fund Calculator helps investors estimate how investments may grow over time based on investment amount, expected returns, and duration - for SIPs or lumpsum investments. Useful for planning retirement, children's education, wealth creation, buying a home. Benefits: 1. Helps Plan Financial Goals. 2. Shows the Power of Compounding. 3. Supports Goal-Based Planning. 4. Simplifies Decision-Making. Octaraa provides SIP Calculator, Lumpsum Calculator, CAGR Calculator, and Target Amount Calculator as part of its goal-based calculator suite. FAQ: What is a Mutual Fund Calculator? A tool that estimates future mutual fund investment growth. Are they accurate? They provide estimated projections based on expected returns and inputs. Can beginners use them? Yes, simple and beginner-friendly. Why use these calculators? They help with planning, clarity, and goal-based investing.

SIP Calculator | Estimate Your Mutual Fund Investment Growth
A SIP Calculator helps estimate the future value of regular Systematic Investment Plan investments based on monthly amount, expected return, and duration. Benefits: 1. Helps Plan Financial Goals (retirement, education, home, wealth creation). 2. Shows the Power of Compounding. 3. Improves Financial Clarity. 4. Encourages Disciplined Investing. How it works: enter monthly investment amount, expected annual rate of return, investment duration -> get total invested, expected returns, estimated maturity value. Octaraa's SIP Calculator helps users plan with clarity; the platform also supports goal-based financial calculators, long-term planning tools, FD booking, financial literacy blogs, and family-focused financial planning features. FAQ: What is a SIP Calculator? A tool estimating future value of SIP investments. Is it accurate? Provides estimated projections based on inputs and expected returns. Why use a SIP calculator? Helps with goal-based financial planning and investment clarity. Can beginners use it? Yes.

Why People Remember Losses More Than Gains | The Psychology Behind Money Decisions
Loss aversion: losing ₹1,000 feels worse than gaining ₹1,000 feels good, because losses create stronger emotional impact (fear, regret) than gains. This is a concept in behavioral finance. Why we remember losses more: 1. Strong Emotional Impact (fear, regret, stress). 2. Survival Instinct (evolutionary bias toward avoiding loss/risk). 3. Recency & Repetition Bias (we replay losses in our minds). 4. Regret & "What If" Thinking ("I should have sold earlier"). Effects on financial decisions: selling investments too early during drops, avoiding investing due to fear of loss, holding losing assets too long hoping to recover, missing long-term growth opportunities. Recommendations: focus on long-term goals (short-term losses are normal), use data not emotions, stay consistent (e.g. SIPs reduce impact of volatility), accept market fluctuations as normal. FAQ: Why do losses feel worse than gains? Loss aversion, a common human tendency. Does it affect investing? Yes, can lead to emotional and poor decisions. How can I avoid this? Focus on long-term planning and avoid emotional reactions.

Why Most Families Lose Wealth | Common Financial Mistakes Families Make
Wealth is not created by earnings alone - it depends on how money is managed, planned, and grown. Common mistakes: 1. Lack of Financial Planning - no clear roadmap, disorganized expenses/savings. 2. Excessive Spending - lifestyle inflation reduces savings and weakens long-term wealth creation. 3. Delaying Investments - reduces benefits of compounding. 4. Emotional Financial Decisions - fear/greed/panic (e.g. panic selling) negatively impact wealth. 5. Lack of Financial Literacy - poor understanding of savings, investing, taxes leads to poor decisions or avoiding investing. 6. No Goal-Based Planning - saving without clear goals leads to inconsistent financial behavior. Recommendations: set clear financial goals (education, retirement, emergency savings, home ownership), track spending and planning, start investing early (time matters for compounding), improve financial literacy, plan as a family (shared discussions improve transparency and coordination). Octaraa helps via financial calculators (SIP returns, savings, long-term wealth potential), FD booking, organizing planning for multiple family members, and financial literacy blogs. FAQ: Why do families struggle financially? Poor planning, overspending, lack of financial literacy. Is high income enough to build wealth? No, depends on planning, saving, investing habits. Biggest mistake families make? Delaying financial planning and investing. How can families improve stability? Financial literacy, goal-based planning, disciplined money management.

Family Financial Planning Platform in India | Manage Your Family's Wealth Smartly
Octaraa helps families take control of their financial journey via centralized planning: plan financial goals together, make data-driven decisions. Benefits: 1. Better Financial Visibility - all family investments/goals in one unified dashboard. 2. Goal-Based Planning - define/track/achieve milestones (education, home, retirement). 3. Smarter Investment Decisions - tracking, projections, tools to optimize investments. 4. Secure Financial Management - data kept safe and organized. Key Features of Octaraa: Family-Centric Wealth Management (manage finances for spouse, parents, children in one place), Financial Calculators (plan savings/investments/goals with precision), Goal & Family Tree Planning (align financial goals across generations). Who should use: young families planning their future, working professionals, parents planning for children's education, individuals managing finances for entire household. How It Works: Step 1 - Sign In & Do KYC (PAN, Aadhaar, personal info). Step 2 - Add Family Members to family tree. Step 3 - Set Goals using calculators. Step 4 - Grow Wealth via dashboard and investments. FAQ: What is a family financial planning platform? Helps manage and track entire family's finances in one place. Is Octaraa suitable for beginners? Yes, designed to be simple and user-friendly. Can I track all family investments together? Yes, manage investments for all family members.

Why Financial Calculators are Important in 2026 | Best Financial Calculators for Smart Planning in India
Financial calculators help make data-driven decisions: estimate investment returns, plan savings goals, calculate loan EMIs, track financial growth. Importance: 1. Accurate Financial Planning. 2. Goal-Based Decision Making (education, retirement, home purchase). 3. Better Investment Strategy. 4. Time-Saving Tools. 5. Smarter Financial Decisions (reduce risk). Octaraa's calculator suite (all "Check it out here!" linking to /resources/*): College Cost Calculator (estimate future education expenses including tuition and living costs), SIP Calculator (estimate growth of regular investments), Step-Up SIP Calculator (shows benefit of increasing SIP amount yearly), Lumpsum Calculator (future value of one-time investment), Target Amount SIP (monthly investment needed to reach a goal, e.g. ₹10 lakh), Cost of Delay Calculator (wealth lost by starting late), Education Loan EMI Calculator, Fixed Deposit Calculator (maturity amount and interest), Recurring Deposit Calculator, SWP Calculator (estimate how long corpus lasts with regular withdrawals), XIRR Calculator (annualized return with multiple cash flows), Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) Calculator (government-backed savings scheme for girl child, tax benefits), PPF Calculator (Public Provident Fund, government-backed, Section 80C tax benefits), Income Tax Calculator (old vs new regime comparison for FY 2025-26), CAGR Calculator (compound annual growth rate). Who should use: young families, students planning education, working professionals, investors, business owners, first-time investors, anyone planning finances. How to use: Step 1 Choose a calculator from the Calculators section. Step 2 Enter Details (investment amount, duration, expected returns, loan rate). Step 3 Get Results instantly. Step 4 Analyze Data (compare scenarios). Step 5 Plan Better (adjust investment/timeline/strategy). FAQ: What are financial calculators? Tools that calculate investments, savings, loan details. Are they accurate? Yes, reliable estimates based on inputs. Which calculator first? Start with SIP or fixed deposits. Are they free? Yes, all free.

Joint vs Individual Investments: Which is Better for You?
Individual investments: made in a single person's name, full control, simple management, clear ownership, easy tax filing. Best for: individuals managing own finances, beginners, people preferring independence. Joint investments: made in name of two or more individuals (typically spouses/family), shared ownership, combined financial planning, easier wealth transfer, better coordination for family goals. Best for: married couples, family financial planning, long-term shared goals. When to choose individual: full control desired, personal goals, simple independent setup, just starting out. When to choose joint: shared financial goals (home, children's education, retirement), better coordination/transparency, long-term family wealth planning, smoother succession/asset transfer. Best Approach (Smart Strategy): combination of both - individual investments for personal goals, joint investments/planning for shared family goals. Octaraa helps manage both via family goal planning and family tree structure, financial calculators for informed decisions, financial literacy blogs. FAQ: Is joint investment better than individual? Depends on goals - both have advantages. Can I have both? Yes, combination is often best strategy. Are joint investments safe? Yes, if planned and managed properly. Who controls joint investments? Depends on how structured.

FAQ - What is Octaraa and how does it help families?
Octaraa is a family-centric financial planning platform that helps you manage, track, and grow your entire family's wealth in one place with smart tools.

FAQ - How is Octaraa different from other financial platforms?
Unlike traditional platforms, Octaraa focuses on family-based financial management, allowing you to manage finances for your spouse, parents, and children from a single unified dashboard.

FAQ - Can I manage my entire family's finances on Octaraa?
Yes, Octaraa allows you to manage investments, track goals, and monitor financial progress for all family members in one place.

FAQ - Is Octaraa suitable for young families in India?
Absolutely, Octaraa is designed specifically for young families in India to help them plan finances, manage financial goals, and build long-term wealth efficiently.

FAQ - What features does Octaraa offer?
Octaraa provides family wealth management, financial calculators, AI-powered financial assistant Samaira AI, goal-based planning.

FAQ - How does Octaraa's AI (Samaira AI) help users?
Octaraa's Samaira AI is a financial assistant which helps you learn financial concepts. Samaira AI was created specifically to enhance financial literacy in India.

FAQ - Are Octaraa's financial calculators useful for planning?
Yes, Octaraa's financial calculators help users plan investments, estimate returns, and achieve financial goals with better accuracy.

FAQ - Is my data secure on Octaraa?
Yes, Octaraa uses advanced security and encryption technologies to ensure your financial data remains safe and protected. Octaraa is hosted on the Google Cloud Platform which is safe, secure & reliable.

FAQ - Do I need financial knowledge to use Octaraa?
No, Octaraa is beginner-friendly and offers financial calculators & Samaira AI, our financial literacy bot, to help users manage finances easily.

FAQ - How can Octaraa help me achieve my family's financial goals?
Octaraa helps you set clear financial goals and track progress, to build and grow your family's wealth over time.

FD options available on Octaraa

FAQ - What is DICGC insurance on fixed deposits?
DICGC (Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation) is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). It provides insurance cover on bank deposits—including Fixed Deposits, Savings Accounts, Current Accounts, and Recurring Deposits. Crucially, DICGC insurance covers both principal and interest amounts up to a maximum limit of ₹5 Lakh per depositor, per bank.

FAQ - How much insurance coverage does DICGC provide?
DICGC provides insurance coverage up to a maximum limit of ₹5 Lakh (including both principal and interest amounts) per depositor, per bank. If you hold deposits across different partner banks, each bank's deposit is insured separately up to the ₹5 Lakh limit.

FAQ - What happens if a bank where I have an FD goes bankrupt?
If a bank where you have a Fixed Deposit goes bankrupt, your deposits are insured by the DICGC (a subsidiary of the RBI) up to a maximum of ₹5 Lakh (including both principal and interest amounts). Any deposits exceeding the ₹5 Lakh limit in that specific bank are not covered by the insurance and would be at risk, subject to the bank's liquidation process.
